hyperedges.label: Execution Stage Plan Task Evidence Contract

nodes.label: Repository Execution Constraints
nodes.rationale: Gateway constraints keep governance files minimal, preserve language boundaries, enforce GitOps-first work, and avoid plaintext Kubernetes secrets.

nodes.label: GitOps Platform Scope
nodes.rationale: The repository is scoped as document SSoT, GitOps manifests, and bootstrap assets rather than an owner of external runtime services.

nodes.label: Lifecycle Hook Boundary
nodes.rationale: The catalog separates repo/static readiness from live or CI evidence and records Stop, SubagentStop, and PreCompact safeguards as policy-driven rather than hook-enforced.

nodes.label: GitOps First Mutation Boundary
nodes.rationale: Agent execution defaults to PR-ready repository changes and static evidence; live mutation is reserved for explicitly approved bootstrap, break-glass, secret rotation, or diagnosis exceptions.

nodes.label: Canonical Document Stage Taxonomy
nodes.rationale: The routing table prevents parallel documentation trees and keeps generated outputs aligned with the repository-native stage taxonomy.

nodes.label: Claude Agent and Codex Mirror Contract
nodes.norm_label: claude agent and codex mirror contract

nodes.label: ADR-0001: k3d Topology and External Network Baseline
nodes.rationale: The ADR fixes a 1 server and 3 agent k3d topology, disables default Traefik, and standardizes external network baselines to improve local reproducibility and external service connectivity, accepting no control-plane HA and IP reference maintenance.
nodes.norm_label: adr-0001: k3d topology and external network baseline

nodes.label: ADR-0002: ArgoCD Helm Install with App-of-Apps and ApplicationSet
nodes.rationale: The ADR chooses Helm values management plus App-of-Apps, ApplicationSet, and scoped AppProjects because external Valkey backend configuration and upgrades are easier to reproduce than raw manifests, while requiring Helm schema tracking.
nodes.norm_label: adr-0002: argocd helm install with app-of-apps and applicationset

nodes.label: ADR-0003 ESO Vault Kubernetes Auth for Runtime Secrets
nodes.rationale: The ADR adopts ESO with Vault Kubernetes Auth so GitOps remains declarative without storing plaintext secrets in Git, accepting synchronization failure risk when ESO or Vault integration breaks.
nodes.norm_label: adr-0003 eso vault kubernetes auth for runtime secrets

nodes.label: ESO Vault Kubernetes Auth Runtime Secrets Pattern
nodes.norm_label: eso vault kubernetes auth runtime secrets pattern

nodes.label: ADR-0004: External Service Access Model and ArgoCD Valkey Backend
nodes.rationale: The ADR standardizes PostgreSQL, Valkey, and Vault on Service plus EndpointSlice fixed IPs to provide a consistent Kubernetes interface, DNS discovery, and NetworkPolicy CIDR control, accepting IPAM maintenance and more initial setup.
nodes.norm_label: adr-0004: external service access model and argocd valkey backend

nodes.label: External Endpoint Contract
nodes.rationale: Vault/ESO integration issues and ArgoCD ingress regressions were attributed to contract mismatch, so fixed external service DNS, IP, port, TLS, and egress contracts were recorded.

nodes.label: Static Contract CI Gates
nodes.rationale: A single pre-commit job could miss contract and workflow security regressions, so path-aware static gates were made mandatory.

nodes.label: ArgoCD Pull Reconciliation Model
nodes.rationale: The platform keeps CD responsibility in ArgoCD pull reconciliation and rejects GitHub Actions push deploy to avoid duplicated deployment authority.

nodes.label: AppProject and Vault Minimum Privilege Controls
nodes.rationale: Namespace wildcards and broad Vault policy wildcards were rejected in favor of allow-lists and required secret paths.
nodes.norm_label: appproject and vault minimum privilege controls

nodes.label: mkcert CA ClusterIssuer
nodes.rationale: Using the already trusted mkcert rootCA lets cert-manager issue trusted local TLS certificates without extra browser setup.

nodes.label: cert-manager TLS Automation
nodes.rationale: Manual mkcert Secret injection does not scale as more ingress endpoints are added, so new services use Certificate CRs.

nodes.label: Kubernetes Dashboard v3 Installation
nodes.rationale: The historical platform expansion required a web UI for workload status and selected the chart-based v3 dashboard for GitOps management.

nodes.label: Headlamp Replacement for Kubernetes Dashboard
nodes.rationale: Dashboard v3 pulled in Kong Gateway, consumed more resources, and its chart repository returned 404, so Headlamp became the current UI contract.

nodes.label: Istio Default Sidecar Mesh
nodes.rationale: The platform needs service mesh features while keeping resource usage bounded in WSL2, so Istio default sidecar mode with namespace opt-in was selected.

nodes.label: ingress-nginx and Istio Coexistence
nodes.rationale: Istio IngressGateway was disabled to avoid port conflicts and preserve the existing ingress-nginx external exposure path.

nodes.label: Kiali External Observability Stack
nodes.rationale: Prometheus, Grafana, and Tempo already run on Docker infra_net, so Kiali integrates with external endpoints instead of deploying duplicate in-cluster observability services.

nodes.label: Kiali Observability Egress Policy
nodes.rationale: Kiali requires egress to the external Prometheus, Grafana, and Tempo address range to visualize mesh topology and telemetry.

nodes.label: Argo Rollouts Progressive Delivery
nodes.rationale: The platform needed canary and blue-green strategies plus Prometheus-based promotion or abort beyond plain ArgoCD Deployment sync.

nodes.label: Manual Promotion Strategy
nodes.rationale: Manual approval was selected as the initial promotion strategy, leaving automatic analysis metrics outside the first installation scope.

nodes.label: Argo Notifications Slack Webhook
nodes.rationale: GitOps sync, health, and Rollouts events need automatic operator notification, and ArgoCD already includes a Notifications controller.

nodes.label: Vault-backed Slack Token
nodes.rationale: The Slack token is stored in Vault and synchronized by ESO into the ArgoCD notifications Secret instead of being committed to Git.

nodes.label: README Template
nodes.rationale: Defines the shared minimum README structure and optional snippets so repository entry documents stay consistent.

nodes.label: Immediate Execution
nodes.author: {team-or-person}
nodes.rationale: The runbook template states that runbooks exist for immediate execution, not policy definition or tutorial-first guidance.

nodes.label: GitOps-first Principle
nodes.source_location: ## How to Work in This Area

nodes.label: Stage Usage Criteria
nodes.rationale: Not every change needs the full documentation chain, but requirements, design judgment, execution evidence, and operations procedure must remain traceable.

nodes.label: External Secrets and Vault contract
nodes.rationale: Secret values stay outside Git and are wired through Vault-backed ExternalSecret resources.

nodes.label: Istio meshConfig defaultConfig
nodes.rationale: holdApplicationUntilProxyStarts is set to false in the istiod Helm values.

nodes.label: ArgoCD Metrics NodePort Services
nodes.rationale: Comments state Docker Prometheus at 172.18.0.10 collects ArgoCD metrics through the k3d node at 172.18.0.2 using NodePorts 30082-30086.

nodes.label: In-cluster Kubernetes log collection
nodes.rationale: Alloy runs in the k3d/k3s cluster so it can collect pod logs through the Kubernetes API; the external Docker Alloy cannot see containerd pod logs.

nodes.label: Kubernetes object state metrics
nodes.rationale: kube-state-metrics exposes kube_pod, kube_deployment, kube_node, and similar Kubernetes object-state metrics; NodePort 30091 is provided for external Docker Prometheus scraping.

nodes.label: NetworkPolicy allow-egress-apps
nodes.rationale: Allows egress from all pods in the apps namespace, currently adminer, to PostgreSQL, DNS, Istiod, and in-cluster pod-to-pod traffic.

nodes.label: Kubernetes API server
nodes.rationale: The policy targets the post-DNAT k3s server address because kube-router evaluates NetworkPolicy after ClusterIP DNAT.

nodes.label: NetworkPolicy allow-egress-monitoring
nodes.rationale: Allows monitoring pods such as alloy-k8s-logs and kube-state-metrics to send logs, watch Kubernetes API resources, resolve DNS, and communicate within the cluster.

nodes.label: AnalysisTemplate adminer-stability
nodes.rationale: The canary analysis measures adminer container restarts; zero restarts succeeds, any restart fails the analysis and triggers rollback.

nodes.label: Istio mTLS STRICT
nodes.rationale: PeerAuthentication applies STRICT mTLS to all pods in apps; the comments note ingress-nginx also needs Istio sidecar injection or ingress-to-adminer traffic is rejected.

nodes.label: GitOps and ArgoCD reconciliation
nodes.rationale: Normal operational changes are handled through GitOps declarations and ArgoCD reconciliation rather than live cluster mutation.

nodes.label: bootstrap-local.sh
nodes.rationale: The bootstrap entrypoint creates initial resources before ArgoCD ownership exists, making it a bootstrap-only exception to normal GitOps operation.

nodes.label: bootstrap-only kubectl apply exception
nodes.rationale: The exception is limited to initial namespace, secret, MetalLB, and root GitOps application creation before ArgoCD owns desired state.

nodes.label: repo-backed static validation utilities
nodes.rationale: The scripts provide repeatable repository-backed checks without replacing GitOps manifests or live runtime validation.

nodes.label: live cluster mutation exclusion
nodes.rationale: The scripts area excludes kubectl apply, kubectl patch, deployment scripts, and direct external runtime changes.

nodes.label: live evidence boundary
nodes.rationale: Static validation does not prove live k3d, Kubernetes API, ArgoCD reconciliation, or external Vault state.

nodes.label: Traefik dynamic config examples
nodes.rationale: These examples are auxiliary local access references; canonical deployment remains GitOps and ArgoCD.

nodes.label: canonical GitOps and ArgoCD deployment path
nodes.norm_label: canonical gitops and argocd deployment path

nodes.label: cloud-native ingress paths
nodes.rationale: AWS and Azure examples are routed to ALB, Gateway API, or AGC instead of extending the local Traefik directory for cloud targets.
